description: Extract structured fields, text, and links from local/static HTML using Scrapling's parser for Hermes research and source operationalization workflows.

verify.command:
C:/Python314/python -c "from scrapling.parser import Adaptor; page=Adaptor('

Hermes Scrapling Smoke

'); print(page.css('h1')[0].get_all_text(separator=' ', strip=True))"

input.html:
Hermes Scrapling Smoke

Local parser proof.

Example

whenToUse: Use when Hermes has static HTML or a saved local HTML file and needs structured extraction of fields, text, or links before research summarization or source operationalization.
failureModes: Scrapling is not installed in the configured Python environment.
The sourcePath does not exist or is not readable.
The HTML is malformed or selectors match no nodes.
URL fetching is requested; S1 intentionally does not support network fetches.
The extraction exceeds the bounded timeout.
